mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising industrial production reflects peak capacity utilization and strong corporate output, pushing S&P 500 returns higher over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases yield negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 150,000 signal robust economic expansion and rising wage income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS surges above 150,000 are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm broad-based demand recovery, lifting equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Combined positive GDPC1 and RSAFS readings fail to predict positive 20-day returns with more than 50% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase discount rates and compress equity multiples, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increases during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances.
